sutta: the buddha said monks possessed of 4 things a man is to be understood as being unworthy what for here in monks the unworthy man even unasked speaks out what is discreditable to another what then would he say if asked if however he is obliged to speak on being questioned then without reserve or equivocation he utters this praise of another to the full and in all details this month is to be understood as the meaning of the saying this worthy is an unworthy 1 then again the unworthy 1 even when asked does not speak out what is to another's credit much less when asked if however he is obliged to speak on being questioned then with reserve and equivocation the artists praise of another grudgingly and in brief this month is to be understood as the meaning of the saying this wordy is an unworthy 1 again monks the unworthy 1 even when asked does not speak out what is discreditable to himself much less does he do so when unasked if however he is obliged to speak on being questioned then with reserve and equivocation he utters grudgingly and in brief what is to his own discredit this monks is to be understood as the meaning of the saying this worthy is an unworthy 1 once more monks the unworthy 1 even unasked speak up speaks out what is creditable to himself what then does he do when asked if however he is obliged to speak on being questioned then without reserve or equivocation he sings his own praises to the full and in all details this month is to be understood as the meaning of the saying is worthy is an unworthy 1 thus possessed of these 4 things 1 is to be understood as an unworthy 1 and vice versa for a worthy person so in this utah the buddha is saying an unworthy person is 1 who is too quick to speak ill of another's faults and he's not ready to speak well of another's good qualities but of his own if he has faults he's always trying to hide his faults and if he's got some good points he'll blow his own trumpet that is an unworthy person but 1 thing we must know that there is a difference in saying what is wrong and talking bad about someone on a personal level there's a difference between pointing out what is right and what is wrong on an impersonal level and speaking bad about somebody on a personal level there is a difference for example in 1 of the earliest uttars we read 1.11.1 the buddha said now what is not dharma should be pointed out now it's not dhamma if somebody teaches something which is not according to the dhamma and he claims that it is according to the dhamma and we know that it is not according to the dhamma then we should point it out now that it is not according to the dhamma otherwise it will harm many people if they learn the wrong dharma if we point out what is right dharma and what is wrong dharma then it will benefit many people a little later we will see another suta [Music] nikaya
